prerequisites: A Google account with access to the Google Classroom data you want to extract.
A Google Cloud project with the Google Classroom API enabled.
An OAuth 2.0 client ID and client secret created in the Google Cloud Console.
A client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token to provide to the connector (all three are required).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Google Classroom authenticates with OAuth 2.0. The connector requires the OAuth client_id and client_secret from your Google Cloud OAuth client, plus a client_refresh_token, which the connector exchanges with Google's authorization server for short-lived access tokens used to call the Classroom REST API.

setupSteps.title: Create a Google Cloud project and enable the Classroom API
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, create or select a project, then enable the Google Classroom API for that project so it can be called with OAuth credentials.

setupSteps.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen and scopes
setupSteps.body: Configure the OAuth consent screen for your project and select the Classroom authorization scopes your extraction needs (for example https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.courses or https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.rosters). Request only the scopes you require. If your app is public and uses scopes that access certain user data, complete Google's app verification process.

setupSteps.title: Create an OAuth 2.0 client ID and client secret
setupSteps.body: In the project's Credentials page, create an OAuth 2.0 client. Google issues a client ID and client secret; supply these as the connector's client_id and client_secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Complete the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow with your client to consent to the chosen Classroom scopes and exchange the resulting authorization code for a refresh token. Provide this value as the connector's client_refresh_token field.

sources.title: Choose Google Classroom API scopes (authentication guide)
Using OAuth 2.0 to Access Google APIs
